# Day 32 AM – Decision Trees & Random Forest

**Week 6 | Machine Learning & AI Take-Home Assignment**

---

## Overview

This repository contains the solution for the Day 32 AM take-home assignment covering:
- Decision Trees (Gini impurity, pruning, rule extraction)
- Random Forest (bagging, feature importance, hyperparameter tuning)
- ExtraTreesClassifier (stretch problem)
- Bias-variance tradeoff, overfitting curves, model comparison infographic

---

## Repository Structure

```
d32-decision-trees-randomforest/
│
├── D32_AM_DT_RandomForest.ipynb     # Main Jupyter notebook (all parts A–D)
├── extra_trees_comparison.md        # Part B research doc – ExtraTrees vs RF
├── README.md                        # This file
│
└── outputs/                         # Saved plots (auto-generated by notebook)
    ├── decision_tree_viz.png
    ├── model_comparison.png
    ├── feature_importance.png
    ├── bias_variance_diagram.png
    ├── overfitting_curve.png
    ├── extra_trees_comparison.png
    └── model_infographic.png
```

---

## How to Run

### 1. Clone the repo
```bash
git clone https://github.com/shubhamrangdal2001-hash/Assignment_Day32AM.git
cd d32-decision-trees-randomforest
```

### 2. Install dependencies
```bash
pip install numpy pandas matplotlib scikit-learn jupyter
```

### 3. Launch the notebook
```bash
jupyter notebook D32_AM_DT_RandomForest.ipynb
```

Run all cells from top to bottom. The notebook will:
- Generate the synthetic loan dataset
- Train and compare Decision Tree and Random Forest
- Output all plots to the `outputs/` folder
- Run the Extra Trees benchmark

---

## Assignment Parts

| Part | Description | Location |
|------|-------------|----------|
| A | Loan approval system – DT rules, RF tuning, feature importance, recommendation | Notebook |
| B | Extra Trees research and benchmark | `extra_trees_comparison.md` + Notebook |
| C | Interview Q&A – bias-variance, overfitting curve, debug exercise | Notebook |
| D | AI-augmented infographic – DT vs RF vs Logistic Regression | Notebook |

---

## Dependencies

- Python 3.8+
- numpy
- pandas
- matplotlib
- scikit-learn >= 1.2

---

## Notes

- `random_state=42` is set throughout for reproducibility.
- All synthetic data is generated fresh on each run — no external datasets required.
- Plots are saved automatically; no manual export needed.
